content.description:
A visual tool for creating and editing Hobson types. Generates type definitions with paired readonly and editable views (generic-view delegates) from a form interface.

## Creating a Type

Fill in the form fields:

- **Type Name** — kebab-case identifier (e.g. `recipe`, `bookmark`)
- **Extends** — parent type (defaults to `kernel:item`)
- **Description** — free-text description stored on the type definition
- **Fields** — each field specifies a content path (`content.title`), a field-view (`text`, `markdown`, `number`, etc.), a mode (`both`, `readonly`, or `editable`), and optional per-field-view settings (placeholder, rows, language, etc.)

A `name` heading field is always auto-included. The summary panel shows what will be created. Click **Create Type** to generate three items (type-def + two views), or **Create Type + Instance** to also create and navigate to a first instance.

After creation the workshop automatically switches to edit mode for the new type.

## Editing an Existing Type

Click **Load Existing** to open a searchable picker of all type definitions. Selecting one populates the form by reverse-engineering the type's generic-view-delegate views:

- Fields, modes, and options are reconstructed from `ui_hints`
- Unrecognized hint properties are preserved for round-trip fidelity
- Custom (non-generic-view) views are listed in a warning banner and left untouched

After loading, the header shows which type is being edited, and the action buttons change to **Update Type** / **Update Type + Instance**. Updates modify the existing type-def and view items in place. Click **New Type** to return to create mode.

## Scope

- Manages generic-view delegates only — hand-written views are left untouched and listed in a warning
- Fields that exist on a type but aren't expressed in ui_hints (e.g. content stored by custom views) won't appear in the form
